sentences.text: This chapter describes the RISC-V ISA extension naming scheme that is used to concisely describe the set of instructions present in a hardware implementation, or the set of instructions used by an application binary interface (ABI).
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions

sentences.text: The ISA naming strings are case insensitive.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Case Sensitivity

sentences.text: RISC-V ISA strings begin with either RV32I, RV32E, RV64I, or RV64E, indicating the supported address space size in bits for the base integer ISA.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Base Integer ISA

sentences.text: Standard ISA extensions are given a name consisting of a single letter.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: For example, the first four standard extensions to the integer bases are: "M" for integer multiplication and division, "A" for atomic memory instructions, "F" for single-precision floating-point instructions, and "D" for double-precision floating-point instructions.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Any RISC-V instruction-set variant can be succinctly described by concatenating the base integer prefix with the names of the included extensions, e.g., "RV64IMAFD".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: We have also defined an abbreviation "G" to represent the "IMAFDZicsrZifencei" base and extensions, as this is intended to represent our standard general-purpose ISA.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard extensions to the RISC-V ISA are given other reserved letters, e.g., "Q" for quad-precision floating-point, or "C" for the 16-bit compressed instruction format.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Some ISA extensions depend on the presence of other extensions, e.g., "D" depends on "F" and "F" depends on "Zicsr".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: These dependencies may be implicit in the ISA name: for example, RV32IF is equivalent to RV32IFZicsr, and RV32ID is equivalent to RV32IFD and RV32IFDZicsr.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Underscores "" may be used to separate ISA extensions to improve readability and to provide disambiguation, e.g., "RV32I2M2A2".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Underscores

sentences.text: Standard unprivileged extensions can also be named by using a single "Z" followed by an alphanumeric name.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: The name must end with an alphabetical character.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: The second letter from the end cannot be numeric if the last letter is "p".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: For example, "Zifencei" names the instruction-fetch fence extension described in .
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: The first letter following the "Z" conventionally indicates the most closely related alphabetical extension category, IMAFDQLCBKJTPVH.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: For the "Zfa" extension for additional floating-point instructions, for example, the letter "f" indicates the extension is related to the "F" standard extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: NOTE: The Zhinx extension is an exception to this rule, as the extension is not related to the H extension but the Zfh extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple "Z" extensions are named, they should be ordered first by category, then alphabetically within a category—for example, "ZicsrZifenceiZtso".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: All multi-letter extensions, including those with the "Z" prefix, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore, e.g., "RV32IMACZicsrZifencei".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Additional Standard Unprivileged Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard extensions that extend the user-level architecture are prefixed with the letters "Su".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > User-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard user-level extensions should be listed after standard unprivileged extensions, and like other multi-letter extensions, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > User-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple user-level extensions are listed, they should be ordered alphabetically.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > User-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard extensions that extend the supervisor-level virtual-memory architecture are prefixed with the letters "Sv", followed by an alphanumeric name.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Other standard extensions that extend the supervisor-level architecture are prefixed with the letters "Ss", followed by an alphanumeric name.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: The name must end with an alphabetical character.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: The second letter from the end cannot be numeric if the last letter is "p".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: These extensions are further defined in Volume II.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: The extensions "sv32", "sv39", "sv48", and "sv59" were defined before the rule against extension names ending in numbers was established.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard supervisor-level extensions should be listed after standard unprivileged and user-level extensions, and like other multi-letter extensions, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple supervisor-level extensions are listed, they should be ordered alphabetically.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Supervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard extensions that extend the hypervisor-level architecture are prefixed with the letters "Sh".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Hypervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard hypervisor-level extensions should be listed after standard unprivileged, user-level and supervisor-level extensions, and like other multi-letter extensions, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Hypervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple hypervisor-level extensions are listed, they should be ordered alphabetically.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Hypervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: NOTE: Many augmentations to the hypervisor-level architecture are more naturally defined as supervisor-level extensions, following the scheme described in the previous section.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Hypervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: The "Sh" prefix is used by the few hypervisor-level extensions that have no supervisor-visible effects.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Hypervisor-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard machine-level instruction-set extensions are prefixed with the letters "Sm".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Machine-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Standard machine-level extensions should be listed after standard lesser-privileged extensions, and like other multi-letter extensions, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Machine-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple machine-level extensions are listed, they should be ordered alphabetically.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Machine-Level Instruction-Set Extension Names

sentences.text: Non-standard extensions are named by using a single "X" followed by the alphanumeric name.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: The name must end with an alphabetic character.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: The second letter from the end cannot be numeric if the last letter is "p".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: For example, "Xhwacha" names the Hwacha vector-fetch ISA extension cite:[hwacha-esscirc2014].
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: Non-standard extensions must be listed after all standard extensions, and, like other multi-letter extensions, must be separated from other multi-letter extensions by an underscore.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: If multiple non-standard extensions are listed, they should be ordered alphabetically.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: For example, an ISA with non-standard extensions Argle and Bargle may be named "RV64IZifenceiXargleXbargle".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Non-Standard Extension Names

sentences.text: Recognizing that instruction sets may expand or alter over time, we encode extension version numbers following the extension name.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: Version numbers are divided into major and minor version numbers, separated by a "p".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: If the minor version is "0", then "p0" can be omitted from the version string.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: To avoid ambiguity, no extension name may end with a number or a "p" preceded by a number.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: Because the "P" extension for Packed SIMD can be confused for the decimal point in a version number, it must be preceded by an underscore if it follows another extension with a version number.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: For example, "rv32i2p2" means version 2.2 of RV32I, whereas "rv32i2p2" means version 2.0 of RV32I with version 2.0 of the P extension.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: Changes in major version numbers imply a loss of backwards compatibility, whereas changes in only the minor version number must be backwards-compatible.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: For example, the original 64-bit standard ISA defined in release 1.0 of this manual can be written in full as "RV64I1p0M1p0A1p0F1p0D1p0", more concisely as "RV64I1M1A1F1D1".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: We define the default version of ratified standard extensions as follows:
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: Version 2 if the extension is one of the following extensions: I, E, M, A, F, D, Q, C, Zicntr, Zicsr, Zifencei, Zihintpause, Zihpm. - Version 1 otherwise.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: For instance, "RV32IV" is equivalent to "RV32I2V1".
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Version Numbers

sentences.text: In canonical ISA name strings, components appear in the following order:
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Canonical Order

sentences.text: One-letter extensions, MAFDQCBPVH, in this order . Z-prefixed extensions .
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Canonical Order

sentences.text: Sm-prefixed extensions . X-prefixed extensions
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Canonical Order

sentences.text: For instance, RV32IMACV is canonical, whereas RV32IMAVC is not.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Canonical Order

sentences.text: summarizes the standardized extension names.
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: .Standard ISA extension names. | |Subset |Name |Implies
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Standard Unprivileged Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Integer Multiplication and Division |M |Zmmul
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Single-Precision Floating-Point |F |Zicsr
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Double-Precision Floating-Point |D |F
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |General |G |IMAFDZicsrZifencei
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Quad-Precision Floating-Point |Q |D
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |16-bit Compressed Instructions |C |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Additional Standard Unprivileged Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Unprivileged extension "abc" |Zabc |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Standard User-Level Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |User-level extension "def" |Sudef |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Standard Supervisor-Level Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Supervisor-level extension "ghi" |Ssghi | |Supervisor-level virtual-memory extension "jkl" |Svjkl |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Standard Hypervisor-Level Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Hypervisor-level extension "mno" |Shmno |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Standard Machine-Level Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Machine-level extension "pqr" |Smpqr |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: 3+^|*Non-Standard Extensions*
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary

sentences.text: |Non-standard extension "stu" |Xstu | |
sentences.section: Preamble > ISA Extension Naming Conventions > Summary
